candidate: MULTILAYER-DETERRENT-EVAL: 5-layer cost-escalating eval cascade (regex/keyword surface check -> small classifier -> embedding-similarity -> mid-size judge -> frontier LLM-as-judge last resort), mirroring velvet-ant escalating defense topology.

calibration_anchors.kw_rule: kw>=2 content-word matches across LLM-side and source-side

per_result_judgments.note: Source-domain anchor: matches >=2 source-side content words about velvet-ant aposematic defense; sem/func near zero because it is biology not LLM evaluation. Counts as hit by kw rule (source-side overlap).
Trust-or-Escalate (ICLR 2025): confidence-based escalation cascade from small to stronger judge — direct functional twin of the cheap-to-expensive aspect, just not explicitly 5 layers.
Decision-theoretic LLM cascade: cost-quality cheap-then-expensive — same backbone idea as the cost-escalating eval cascade.
Cost-effective LLM-as-judge: addresses the same cost-vs-judge-quality optimization without explicit 5-layer cascade.
Multi-agent debate for safety eval: multi-stage evaluation pipeline; similar layered evaluation but debate not strict cost-cascade.
Judge reliability harness — about stress testing judges, not cost-escalating cascade. Below all three thresholds.
Direct twin: explicit 5-layer regex->classifier->embedding->mid-judge->frontier cascade — matches the candidate's distinctive mechanism almost exactly.
Aposematic surface deflection + last-resort frontier judge — explicitly the candidate's distinctive surface-plus-last-resort framing.

summary: 7 of 8 retrieved sources hit on the cost-escalating LLM judge cascade theme via at least one of the three rules, including two near-direct twins (ranks 7 and 8) that match the explicit 5-layer cascade and the aposematic surface + last-resort frontier framing. The candidate's distinctive contribution (explicit velvet-ant-style 5-layer cascade with aposematic surface deflection) is already covered by prior/concurrent work, so MULTILAYER-DETERRENT-EVAL is high-overlap and not novel.
